messages.content: edit: This groundbreaking legislation has propelled Germany to the forefront of digital health policy, not only by encouraging the use of digital health solutions but also by facilitating the utilization of health data for research purposes.

Edit: This groundbreaking legislation has positioned Germany as a frontrunner in digital health policy, as it not only promotes the adoption of digital health solutions but also enables the efficient utilization of health data for research purposes.

edit: Furthermore, Germany’s statutory health insurance system is one of the largest globally, providing coverage for around 90% of the population, approximately 75 million people. With only 10% of the population being privately insured, conducting research in Germany provides an ample and representative context for studying digital health, particularly in the context of a cross-country study.

Edit: Additionally, Germany's exemplary statutory health insurance system is regarded as one of the largest on a global scale, catering to approximately 90% (around 75 million) of the population. With private insurance covering only 10% of the population, conducting research in Germany offers a significant and representative context for studying digital health, making it an ideal choice for cross-country studies.

edit: Furthermore, Germany’s statutory health insurance system is one of the largest globally, providing coverage for around 90% of the population, approximately 75 million people. With only 10% of the population being privately insured, conducting research in Germany provides an ample and representative context for studying digital health, particularly in the context of a cross-country study.

Edit: Additionally, Germany’s expansive statutory health insurance system covers approximately 90% of the population, equivalent to around 75 million people. In contrast, only 10% of the population is privately insured. This significant disparity makes Germany an ideal location for conducting research on digital health, as it offers a diverse and representative context, especially when considering cross-country studies.

EDIT: Furthermore, Germany’s statutory health insurance system is one of the largest globally, providing coverage for around 90% of the population, approximately 75 million people, with only 10% of the population being privately insured.  Conducting research in Germany provides an ample and representative context for studying digital health, particularly in the context of a cross-country study.

Edit: In addition to being one of the largest worldwide, Germany's statutory health insurance system offers coverage to approximately 90% of its population, which translates to around 75 million individuals. By contrast, only 10% of the population has private insurance. This stark contrast not only demonstrates the significance of Germany's healthcare system but also makes it an ideal setting for conducting research on digital health. The country provides a rich and representative context, especially when considering cross-country studies in the field.

sumraize: Therefore, I began my search for a professor at an accredited German university whose research aligns with my interests in digital health. After extensive searching, I discovered that Dr. Till Winkler is a leading professor in digital health research. Dr. Winkler holds the Chair of Information Management at the University of Hagen.

I sought a professor at a renowned German university who focuses on digital health research and came across Dr. Till Winkler. He is a prominent figure in this field and holds the prestigious Chair of Information Management at the University of Hagen.

sumarize: His research has been published in reputable journals such as the Journal of Management Information Systems, Journal of the Association of Information Systems, MIS Quarterly Executive, and Health Policy & Technology. Dr. Winkler has been recognized with the Early Career Award from the Association of Information Systems. He also serves in editorial roles, including as an Associate Editor for Business and Information Systems Engineering (BISE). Furthermore, he has a proven track record of successfully acquiring and managing third-party funding for research projects.

Dr. Till Winkler has an impressive record of published research in prominent journals like the Journal of Management Information Systems, Journal of the Association of Information Systems, MIS Quarterly Executive, and Health Policy & Technology. He has received recognition for his work with the Early Career Award from the Association of Information Systems. Additionally, Dr. Winkler plays a role in the editorial process as an Associate Editor for Business and Information Systems Engineering (BISE). Furthermore, he has a strong expertise in acquiring and managing third-party funding for research projects.

Dr. Till Winkler is a respected researcher in digital health with publications in reputable journals such as the Journal of Management Information Systems, Journal of the Association of Information Systems, MIS Quarterly Executive, and Health Policy & Technology. He has received recognition for his work, including the Early Career Award from the Association of Information Systems. Dr. Winkler also serves as an Associate Editor for Business and Information Systems Engineering (BISE) and has a proven track record in securing and managing third-party funding for research projects.

improve and sumrize: With this in mind, I contacted Dr. Till Winkler to express my interest in a research opportunity in the field of digital health. Dr. Winkler evaluated my qualifications and determined that I am well-suited to conduct a research project in digital health after conducting an interview and carefully considering my expertise and skills. So, I prepared a research proposal focused on digital health adoption titled “The Influence of Long-Term Orientation on Patient Attitudes Towards Digital Health: A Cross-Country Study in Germany and Iran.” Fortunately, it was approved and I awarded a fund of 13,104 Euro. As a result, I commenced my funded research project at the university of Hagen in July 2022.

I contacted Dr. Till Winkler to express my interest in a digital health research opportunity and he thoroughly assessed my qualifications. After conducting an interview and carefully considering my expertise and skills, Dr. Winkler determined that I am well-suited to conduct a research project in this field. Encouraged by his evaluation, I prepared and submitted a research proposal titled "The Influence of Long-Term Orientation on Patient Attitudes Towards Digital Health: A Cross-Country Study in Germany and Iran." Fortunately, my proposal was approved and I was awarded a fund of 13,104 Euro. Consequently, I commenced my funded research project at the University of Hagen in July 2022.

edit: Fortunately, due to the importance of the project, my project has been further developed so that I am currently working on five important research articles in this field. These articles are expected to continue until the end of August 2024. Due to the importance of continuing these research projects for Hagen University and Hagen University's satisfaction with my work, Hagen University has extended my stay until the end of August 2024.

Edit: Given the significance of my project, I have had the opportunity to expand it further. Currently, I am actively working on five crucial research articles within the field of digital health. These articles are anticipated to be ongoing until August 2024. Recognizing the value of continuing these research endeavors for the University of Hagen, and with their satisfaction regarding my contributions, my stay at the university has been extended until the end of August 2024.
